title: Bridge Essay: Intentional binding for noninvasive BCI control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.

**Intentional binding for noninvasive BCI control.**. J Neural Eng.. PMID: 38996409. DOI: 10.1088/1741-2552/ad628c. Objective. Noninvasive brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) allow to interact with the external environment by naturally bypassing the musculoskeletal system. Making BCIs efficient and accurate is paramount to improve the reliability of real-life and clinical applications, from open-loop device control to closed-loop neurorehabilitation.Approach. By promoting sense of agency and embodiment, realistic setups including multimodal channels of communication, such as eye-gaze, and robotic prostheses aim
